questions.scenario: `orders.devpolaris.com` resolves to an old load balancer address. Which layer is wrong first?

options.text: The DNS record for the public name.
The Cloud Run container port only.

questions.explanation: DNS gives the name an answer. If the answer points to the wrong entry, traffic starts in the wrong place.

questions.scenario: A DNS record points to the intended load balancer. What has DNS not proven yet?

options.text: That the certificate, route, backend, and service health are correct.
That the name has an answer.
That clients can find the next destination.

questions.explanation: DNS only maps the name to the next destination. The rest of the entry path still needs review.

questions.scenario: Which DNS details help with rollout and rollback?

options.text: The zone that owns the name.
The target the record points to.

questions.explanation: Ownership, target, and TTL are the DNS details that shape public entry changes.
